---
title: ""
page-layout: full
format:
  html:
    toc: false
---

```{=html}
<section style="padding:5rem 0 4rem;border-bottom:1px solid #d8dce8;background:#f5f7fc;margin:-1rem -1rem 0">
  <div style="max-width:1100px;margin:0 auto;display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr;gap:4rem;align-items:center;padding:0 2.5rem">
    <div>
      <div style="font-family:'IBM Plex Mono',monospace;font-size:0.7rem;color:#1a56db;letter-spacing:0.16em;text-transform:uppercase;margin-bottom:1.5rem;display:flex;align-items:center;gap:0.625rem">
        <span style="display:block;width:1.5rem;height:1px;background:#1a56db;flex-shrink:0"></span>
        Open-source R packages
      </div>
      <h1 style="font-family:'IBM Plex Serif',serif;font-size:clamp(2.5rem,4vw,3.75rem);font-weight:300;line-height:1.12;margin-bottom:1.375rem">
        Rigorous by<br><em style="color:#1a56db">design.</em>
      </h1>
      <p style="font-size:1.0625rem;color:#68717f;line-height:1.75;margin-bottom:2.25rem;font-weight:300;max-width:480px">
        Five R packages covering the full lifecycle of regulated pharmaceutical statistics — reproducibility, Bayesian methodology, audit logging, data provenance, and estimand specification.
      </p>
      <div style="display:flex;gap:0.875rem;flex-wrap:wrap">
        <a href="#packages" style="background:#1a56db;color:#fff;padding:0.8125rem 1.75rem;border-radius:4px;font-size:0.9375rem;font-weight:500;text-decoration:none">Explore packages</a>
        <a href="https://github.com/repro-stats" target="_blank" style="border:1px solid #d8dce8;color:#68717f;padding:0.8125rem 1.75rem;border-radius:4px;font-size:0.9375rem;text-decoration:none">View on GitHub</a>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div>
      <div style="background:#0f1520;border-radius:10px;overflow:hidden;box-shadow:0 24px 64px rgba(12,17,25,0.16);font-family:'IBM Plex Mono',monospace;font-size:0.8rem;line-height:1.85">
        <div style="background:#1e2530;padding:0.75rem 1rem;display:flex;align-items:center;gap:0.5rem;border-bottom:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.06)">
          <span style="width:12px;height:12px;border-radius:50%;background:#ff5f57;display:inline-block"></span>
          <span style="width:12px;height:12px;border-radius:50%;background:#febc2e;display:inline-block"></span>
          <span style="width:12px;height:12px;border-radius:50%;background:#28c840;display:inline-block"></span>
          <span style="font-size:0.68rem;color:rgba(255,255,255,0.28);margin-left:auto">R console</span>
        </div>
        <div style="padding:1.5rem;color:#e0e8f0">
          <div><span style="color:#00c8a8">&gt;</span> library(<span style="color:#79b8ff">reproducr</span>)</div>
          <div style="color:#64a87e;padding-left:1.25rem">✓ reproducr 0.2.1 loaded</div>
          <br>
          <div><span style="color:#00c8a8">&gt;</span> <span style="color:#79b8ff">audit_script</span>(<span style="color:#9ecbff">"analysis.R"</span>)</div>
          <div style="color:#64a87e;padding-left:1.25rem">✓ 18 functions certified</div>
          <div style="color:#64a87e;padding-left:1.25rem">✓ Risk score: 0.02 (low)</div>
          <br>
          <div><span style="color:#00c8a8">&gt;</span> library(<span style="color:#79b8ff">bayprior</span>)</div>
          <div style="color:#64a87e;padding-left:1.25rem">✓ bayprior 0.1.0 loaded</div>
          <div><span style="color:#00c8a8">&gt;</span> p &lt;- <span style="color:#79b8ff">elicit_beta</span>(mean=0.35, sd=0.10)</div>
          <div style="color:#64a87e;padding-left:1.25rem">✓ Beta(8.2, 15.3) elicited</div>
          <br>
          <div><span style="color:#00c8a8">&gt;</span> <span style="color:#79b8ff">certify</span>(<span style="color:#9ecbff">"submission"</span>)</div>
          <div style="color:#28c840;padding-left:1.25rem">✓ Environment locked — submission ready</div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
  <div style="max-width:1100px;margin:3rem auto 0;padding:2.5rem 2.5rem 0;border-top:1px solid #d8dce8;display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(5,1fr);gap:1.5rem">
    <div><div style="font-family:'IBM Plex Mono',monospace;font-size:1.25rem;font-weight:500;color:#0c1119;margin-bottom:0.25rem">2</div><div style="font-size:0.76rem;color:#9aa2ae;line-height:1.4">packages<br>on CRAN</div></div>
    <div><div style="font-family:'IBM Plex Mono',monospace;font-size:1.25rem;font-weight:500;color:#0c1119;margin-bottom:0.25rem">5</div><div style="font-size:0.76rem;color:#9aa2ae;line-height:1.4">packages in<br>the ecosystem</div></div>
    <div><div style="font-family:'IBM Plex Mono',monospace;font-size:1.25rem;font-weight:500;color:#0c1119;margin-bottom:0.25rem">97%</div><div style="font-size:0.76rem;color:#9aa2ae;line-height:1.4">test coverage<br>in reproducr</div></div>
    <div><div style="font-family:'IBM Plex Mono',monospace;font-size:1.25rem;font-weight:500;color:#0c1119;margin-bottom:0.25rem">0.2.1</div><div style="font-size:0.76rem;color:#9aa2ae;line-height:1.4">reproducr<br>latest version</div></div>
    <div><div style="font-family:'IBM Plex Mono',monospace;font-size:1.25rem;font-weight:500;color:#0c1119;margin-bottom:0.25rem">FDA</div><div style="font-size:0.76rem;color:#9aa2ae;line-height:1.4">2026 Bayesian<br>guidance aligned</div></div>
  </div>
</section>
```

## Packages {#packages}

Five openly developed packages — two on CRAN today, three in active development.

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### `reproducr` <span style="font-family:'IBM Plex Mono',monospace;font-size:0.62rem;background:#e5f5ed;color:#0a7a4a;border:1px solid rgba(10,122,74,0.2);padding:0.2rem 0.55rem;border-radius:3px;font-weight:500;vertical-align:middle">CRAN</span>

The reproducibility foundation. Audit, certify, and monitor R scripts and environments for regulated settings.

- Script-level audit trail with cryptographic hash
- Risk scoring and environment certification
- Lockfiles and drift detection

```r
install.packages("reproducr")
```

[Docs & source →](https://github.com/repro-stats/reproducr){target="_blank"}
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### `bayprior` <span style="font-family:'IBM Plex Mono',monospace;font-size:0.62rem;background:#e5f5ed;color:#0a7a4a;border:1px solid rgba(10,122,74,0.2);padding:0.2rem 0.55rem;border-radius:3px;font-weight:500;vertical-align:middle">CRAN</span> <span style="font-family:'IBM Plex Mono',monospace;font-size:0.62rem;background:#f0e8ff;color:#6b21a8;border:1px solid rgba(107,33,168,0.2);padding:0.2rem 0.55rem;border-radius:3px;font-weight:500;vertical-align:middle">Shiny app</span>

Structured Bayesian prior elicitation, conflict diagnostics, sensitivity analysis, and regulatory reporting. Aligned with FDA 2026 draft guidance.

- SHELF-style elicitation across 6 distribution families
- Prior-data conflict diagnostics
- Regulatory prior justification reports (HTML/PDF/Word)

```r
install.packages("bayprior")
```

[Docs & source →](https://ndohpenngit.github.io/bayprior/){target="_blank"}
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### `regulog` <span style="font-family:'IBM Plex Mono',monospace;font-size:0.62rem;background:#fef0e5;color:#c45a00;border:1px solid rgba(196,90,0,0.18);padding:0.2rem 0.55rem;border-radius:3px;font-weight:500;vertical-align:middle">In development</span>

Electronic audit logging for R, designed around 21 CFR Part 11 requirements.

- Immutable audit log with digital signatures
- User-attributed action tracking
- Deep integration with reproducr

```r
remotes::install_github("repro-stats/regulog")
```

[View on GitHub →](https://github.com/repro-stats/regulog){target="_blank"}
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### `lineager` <span style="font-family:'IBM Plex Mono',monospace;font-size:0.62rem;background:#fef0e5;color:#c45a00;border:1px solid rgba(196,90,0,0.18);padding:0.2rem 0.55rem;border-radius:3px;font-weight:500;vertical-align:middle">In development</span>

Row-level data provenance for clinical datasets. Track every transformation from SDTM to ADaM.

- Row-level transformation tracking
- SDTM to ADaM derivation audit trail
- CDISC-compatible lineage documentation

```r
remotes::install_github("repro-stats/lineager")
```

[View on GitHub →](https://github.com/repro-stats/lineager){target="_blank"}
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### `estimandr` <span style="font-family:'IBM Plex Mono',monospace;font-size:0.62rem;background:#fef0e5;color:#c45a00;border:1px solid rgba(196,90,0,0.18);padding:0.2rem 0.55rem;border-radius:3px;font-weight:500;vertical-align:middle">In development</span>

ICH E9(R1) estimand framework for R. Define, document, and implement your estimand strategy directly in your analysis pipeline. Designed to pair with `bayprior` for Bayesian estimand analyses.

- Structured estimand specification objects
- Intercurrent event strategy documentation
- SAP-ready estimand output tables

```r
remotes::install_github("repro-stats/estimandr")
```

[View on GitHub →](https://github.com/repro-stats/estimandr){target="_blank"}
:::

:::

---

## About {#about}

Reproducibility in clinical analyses is harder than it should be. R environments go undocumented, package versions drift, and the steps between raw data and submission output are rarely captured in a way that supports independent verification.

ReproStats is an open-source project building the R infrastructure to address this. Five packages covering reproducibility and certification (**reproducr**, **regulog**), Bayesian prior methodology (**bayprior**), data provenance (**lineager**), and estimand specification (**estimandr**).

**reproducr** and **bayprior** are on CRAN. The remaining three are in active development. All packages are openly developed on GitHub and designed for GxP environments.

ReproStats is maintained by **Ndoh Penn**, a biostatistician based in Brussels, Belgium. The enterprise platform built on this foundation is [Reprosia](https://reprosia.com){target="_blank"}.

Questions, contributions, or collaboration → [open an issue on GitHub](https://github.com/repro-stats){target="_blank"} or [penn@reprosia.com](mailto:penn@reprosia.com).
